why_them: Castle Chemist is a single-site independent pharmacy in Castle Douglas operating under SH Pharm Ltd with zero web presence — no website, no online prescription ordering, no Google Business profile with a linked site. All digital touchpoints are NHS directory listings only (NHS Inform, NHS 24). Local competitors like Right Medicine Pharmacy have fully featured WordPress and Bookly-integrated booking sites. The pharmacy relies entirely on foot traffic and word of mouth with no mechanism to capture patients relocating to the area or searching online for a local chemist.
recommended_service: New website build with prescription request form, Google Business Profile optimisation, and basic SEO
outreach_hook: I searched for a pharmacy in Castle Douglas online and Castle Chemist doesn't appear with a website — anyone new to the area can't find you. A simple site with an online prescription request form could change that overnight.

why_them: KBT Pharmacy (Tag Healthcare Ltd) is Kirkcudbright's only independent pharmacy and has no website whatsoever — its entire digital footprint is a Facebook page and NHS directory entries. Kirkcudbright is a tourist town with significant seasonal visitor traffic; without a website the pharmacy cannot communicate holiday opening hours, travel clinic services, or prescription delivery to seasonal residents. The NHS email on record is publicly available, making decision-maker access straightforward.
recommended_service: New website with opening hours, services page, online prescription request, and local SEO
outreach_hook: KBT Pharmacy has no website at all — I checked — meaning every tourist and new resident in Kirkcudbright searching 'pharmacy near me' can't find your opening hours or services. That's footfall you're losing every week.

why_them: William Murray Pharmacy (WM Murray Chemist) is a long-established independent in Dumfries with no dedicated website — only directory listings on Yell, Healthera, and NHS Inform. Operating in Dumfries where Boots has a full e-commerce site and Right Medicine Pharmacy has a modern WordPress build with appointment booking, the absence of even a basic web presence means patients searching online for repeat prescriptions or minor ailments are being directed to chain competitors by default. The NHS contact email is publicly listed, making outreach possible.
recommended_service: New website with prescription request form, services overview, and Google Business Profile optimisation
outreach_hook: A quick Google search for pharmacies in Dumfries puts Boots at the top and gives William Murray Pharmacy no website to click — every patient searching online for a local independent chemist is landing on a chain instead.

location: St Michaels Bridge Road, Dumfries, DG1 2PX
why_them: Holm Pharm is an independently owned single-site pharmacy (Holm Pharm Limited) that acquired the historic G.R. Blount premises; despite this heritage story and a motivated independent owner, the pharmacy has no functional website — a previously referenced albapharm subdomain returns DNS not found. The only online presence is a Facebook page and NHS directory entries. No email address is publicly listed, making contact harder, but the owner's entrepreneurial background suggests openness to investment in digital growth.
recommended_service: New WordPress website with brand storytelling, prescription request form, and local SEO
outreach_hook: Holm Pharm's website domain is completely dead — I got a DNS error trying to visit it — so right now anyone searching for you online finds nothing but a Facebook page and an NHS listing.

why_them: Whithorn Pharmacy runs on the proprietary MedicineChest platform served via obfuscated base64-encoded Translator.php URLs, which creates a heavily JavaScript-dependent site with no standard CMS editability — the owner cannot update content without going through the platform vendor. The site has no visible copyright year or blog, the travel clinic pricing section references legacy pandemic-era content (Covid test for travel), and social media presence is absent from the site itself. The NHS email on the public record means a decision-maker is contactable.
recommended_service: Website redesign onto WordPress with editable content, blog, and integrated prescription request
outreach_hook: Your current website runs on a locked proprietary pharmacy platform — you can't update a word of it without going through a third-party vendor, and the travel clinic page still shows Covid test content from the pandemic.

why_them: Welcome Pharmacy is a small independent group (Newton Stewart and Wigtown branches) running on the same proprietary MedicineChest/Translator.php platform as Whithorn Pharmacy — meaning all content is locked behind base64-encoded vendor infrastructure and cannot be independently edited. The site references legacy features including pandemic-era Covid test travel content. The group has no independent SEO strategy, no blog, and no social media integration, leaving organic search almost entirely dependent on NHS directory listings.
recommended_service: Website rebuild onto an editable CMS (WordPress), local SEO, and Google Business Profile management for both branches
outreach_hook: Welcome Pharmacy's website is built on a locked vendor platform — meaning you can't edit your own content — and it still has pandemic-era Covid travel test references that could undermine trust with new patients searching for your services.

location: 20 High Street, Gatehouse of Fleet, DG7 2HP
why_them: Gatehouse Pharmacy is a newly independent pharmacy (Gatehouse Healthcare Ltd) that took over from Boots and is actively building a local health hub model. At launch it has no website, no online prescription capability, and no digital presence beyond a Facebook page — a critical gap for a new business trying to establish patient trust and attract registrations from the former Boots customer base. The timing is ideal: the owner is in growth mode and has explicitly signalled ambition to expand services.
recommended_service: New website build with prescription request form, services overview, appointment booking, and launch SEO
outreach_hook: Gatehouse Pharmacy just launched as an independent and has no website yet — the former Boots patients searching online for their new local pharmacy are finding nothing, and that's registrations walking to the next town.

why_them: Guide Pharmacy (Annan Store Ltd) is an independent pharmacy that took over the former Lloyds Pharmacy on Rose Street in Annan and operates with no website — only a Facebook page and NHS directory listings. The rebrand from a national chain to an independent makes digital presence particularly urgent: former Lloyds patients searching online still find Lloyds Pharmacy branding at that address, creating confusion. Without a dedicated website, Guide Pharmacy cannot communicate its new identity, services, or NHS Pharmacy First capability to the local population.
recommended_service: New website with brand establishment, pharmacy services page, online prescription request, and Google Business Profile update
outreach_hook: Search for Guide Pharmacy in Annan and Google still shows Lloyds Pharmacy branding at your address — without your own website, patients who've heard you've gone independent have nowhere to land online.
